name: Bohonal de Ibor from Belvís de Monroy – circular by Río Tajo
name_original: Bohonal de Ibor desde Belvís de Monroy – circular por río Tajo
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Belvís De Monroy traverse diverse landscapes within the Campo Arañuelo region of Extremadura. The area features undulating steppes, dehesas of holm and cork oaks, and routes along the Tagus River and Valdecañas reservoir. Quiet, scenic roads wind through these natural features, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region also includes parts of the Sierras de Extremadura, providing routes with some elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Belvís De Monroy The most popular touring cycling route is Arrocampo Reservoir Loop from Almaraz, a 25.9 miles (41.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Arrocampo Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bohonal de Ibor from Belvís de Monroy – circular by Río Tajo, a difficult 26.8 miles (43.2 km) path. This route provides views of the Tagus River and passes through varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Monroy Castle Loop from Navalmoral de la Mata, a 20.1 miles (32.3 km) trail leading through the landscape surrounding Belvís de Monroy Castle, often completed in about 2 hours 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Belvís De Monroy is defined by undulating steppes, dehesas, and routes along the Tagus River and Valdecañas reservoir. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes to suit different ability levels. The routes in Belvís De Monroy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 53 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Belvís De Monroy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Belvís De Monroy?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Belvís De Monroy, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find routes traversing diverse landscapes, from riverbanks to dehesas.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Belvís De Monroy?
A: The region offers a varied cycling experience. You can expect quiet, scenic roads winding through undulating steppes like the 'Llanos de Belén,' light forests of holm and cork oaks known as 'dehesas,' and picturesque routes along the Tagus River and Valdecañas reservoir. Some routes also venture into the Sierras de Extremadura, providing more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Belvís De Monroy?
A: Yes, Belvís De Monroy offers several easy touring cycling routes, with 5 routes specifically categorized as easy. The region's quiet roads and the presence of 'Via Verde' cycle paths, converted from former railway lines, provide excellent, often flat options suitable for families and beginners. The 'Senda de los Frailes' is also a well-marked, circular route that can be enjoyed.

Q: What are some long-distance touring cycling options near Belvís De Monroy?
A: While many routes are day trips, the extensive network of quiet roads in Extremadura allows for longer touring experiences. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111890518/bohonal-de-ibor-from-belvis-de-monroy-circular-by-rio-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bohonal de Ibor from Belvís de Monroy – circular by Río Tajo&lt;/a&gt; offer significant distances, and you can combine multiple routes to create extended tours through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical sites. You can explore the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belvís de Monroy&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the Roman heritage at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Temple of the Woodcutters of Augustobriga&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the Valdecañas reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaraz Bridge over the Tagus River&lt;/a&gt; is another notable landmark providing stunning views.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113818113/arrocampo-reservoir-loop-from-almaraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrocampo Reservoir Loop from Almaraz&lt;/a&gt;. Another example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111904297/monroy-castle-loop-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monroy Castle Loop from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the scenic landscape surrounding the castle.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Belvís De Monroy?
A: The region is generally pleasant for outdoor activities. Spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures for touring cycling, with milder weather and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, while winters are generally mild, making cycling possible year-round, though some routes might be more exposed.

Q: Can I expect to see wildlife while cycling in Belvís De Monroy?
A: Absolutely. The 'dehesas' (holm and cork oak forests) and undulating steppes like the 'Llanos de Belén' are rich habitats. Bird watchers often spot species such as Montagu's harrier and bustards. The proximity to Monfragüe National Park, a natural paradise of Mediterranean native forest, further enhances opportunities for wildlife observation.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Belvís De Monroy?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars. Reviewers often praise the quiet, scenic roads, the diverse landscapes ranging from river views to dehesas, and the opportunity to combine cycling with exploring historical landmarks like the Belvís de Monroy Castle.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 9 difficult routes available. These routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113790186/higuera-de-albalat-from-almaraz-circular-by-the-rio-tajo-and-castillo-de-belvis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Higuera de Albalat from Almaraz – circular by the Río Tajo and Castillo de Belvís&lt;/a&gt;, often feature significant elevation changes and longer distances, particularly those venturing into the Sierras de Extremadura.

Q: Are there any unique natural monuments to visit by bike?
A: Yes, a remarkable natural monument in the region is the 'Alcornoque de la Dehesa,' a singular cork oak estimated to be around 250 years old with an impressive 26-meter canopy. Cycling routes in the dehesa areas may offer the opportunity to visit this ancient tree.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Belvís De Monroy?
A: Many routes start from towns like Almaraz or Navalmoral de la Mata, where public parking is generally available. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the komoot tour details for recommended starting points and parking information, which often include designated areas near trailheads or town centers.
tips: A 3.5 m granite jurisdictional landmark dating from the 14th century symbolizes the importance and governance of the city.
Remains of a marble temple from an ancient Roman city called Augustobriga. Spectacular views of the Valdecañas reservoir and the bridge that crosses Guadalupe.
Bridge over the Tagus River and the EX-118 Valdecañas Reservoir road between Bohonal de Ibor and Peraleda de la Mata. You can't walk past without stopping for a photo.
